titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the state's efforts to combat economic abuse in intimate relationships.
html:
This document contains the government's assessment and actions in response to the Swedish National Audit Office's report, "The State's Efforts to Combat Economic Abuse in Close Relationships" (RiR 2025:6). The National Audit Office reviewed whether the state is working effectively to prevent economic abuse in close relationships.

The National Audit Office's overall assessment is that there are significant shortcomings in the state's work. The National Audit Office assesses that the government and relevant authorities have not taken sufficient measures to prevent economic abuse, and that the government has not sufficiently addressed the problem. Furthermore, the National Audit Office assesses that the government has not systematically followed up on the need for legislative measures. The general level of knowledge about the extent and nature of this abuse is also considered to be fragmented.

The government prioritizes work to prevent and combat violence against women, domestic violence, honor-related violence and oppression, as well as prostitution and human trafficking. Preventing economic abuse is central to this work. The government welcomes the National Audit Office's review, which provides an important contribution to the continued development of this work.

The government has taken several measures in line with the National Audit Office's recommendations and will continue the work to prevent and combat economic abuse within the framework of a forthcoming long-term strategy regarding violence against women, domestic violence, honor-related violence and oppression, as well as prostitution and human trafficking.

With this document, the government considers the National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Agency has identified significant shortcomings in how the state works to prevent economic abuse within families. The government will now develop a new plan to improve protection and knowledge regarding this type of abuse.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that reviews how the state performs its duties. They have investigated whether the state is doing enough to prevent financial abuse, which is when a person uses money to control or harm another person in a close relationship. The report shows that there are significant problems and that the state has not done enough to address the situation.

According to the report, the government and other agencies have not done enough to counter this abuse. There is also a lack of good data on the actual extent of the problem. The government has also not adequately assessed whether new laws are needed to prevent this abuse.

The government states that they believe the audit is important for them to improve their work. They are already working to prevent various forms of violence, such as violence against women and human trafficking. Financial abuse is an important part of that work.

Now, the government will create a long-term strategy, which means a long-term plan, to combat all types of violence in close relationships. They have already begun making changes that they are recommended to make following this report.
